title: Name age and household authority remain unresolved
source_observation: The text identifies Aadhirai and her household role but does not establish one English spelling exact age property office or complete marriage chronology
risk: Public materials could invent a fixed biography or treat one transliteration as settled
decision: Retain Aadhirai as the working project form list verified variants and keep age status property and ritual claims provisional
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender history; household economy; publicity

title: Attempted fire death is tragedy not ideal wifehood
source_observation: Aadhirai attempts to enter fire after receiving false news of Sadhuvan's death and the act is interrupted or prevented
risk: The sequence could aestheticise self-destruction or present it as the highest form of marital virtue
decision: Stage social pressure grief bodily danger and interruption without celebratory music erotic fire imagery or moral instruction to imitate her
rationale: Preserves the episode while preventing harmful glorification
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender history; suicide-prevention sensitivity; director; editor

title: Aadhirai has a life beyond waiting
source_observation: The narrative centres marriage loss reunion and alms but does not state that her entire identity is passive fidelity
risk: The adaptation could reduce her to a perfect abandoned wife whose only action is endurance
decision: Show household judgement resource management relationships and the capacity to make an independent public offering
rationale: Creates a playable person and explains why Manimekalai is directed to her
required_review: Screenplay; gender history; household economy; performance

title: Sadhuvan's moral change does not erase household harm
source_observation: Sadhuvan survives shipwreck develops an ethic toward vulnerable survivors and returns after having left or neglected Aadhirai
risk: A conversion-adventure arc could make Aadhirai merely the reward for his improvement
decision: Preserve his change while allowing her memory anger uncertainty boundaries and agency in any reunion
required_review: Classical Tamil; relationship ethics; screenplay; performance

title: Reunion does not require instant forgiveness
source_observation: The text presents return but the exact emotional and procedural content of reconciliation requires verification
risk: A single embrace could erase abandonment false bereavement and unequal risk
decision: Stage reunion as recognition and negotiation and do not assert forgiveness or restored marriage beyond the text
rationale: Maintains emotional truth without fabricating permanent separation
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender review; dialogue; actors

title: The first offering centres women's authority and trust
source_observation: Aadhirai gives the first household offering linked with the vessel's feeding mission
risk: The scene could make her a decorative donor purchasing merit while Manimekalai alone carries agency
decision: Show Aadhirai deciding what leaves the household and entrusting food to Manimekalai's public purpose
rationale: Connects domestic labour and resources to public compassion
required_review: Food history; gender history; classical Tamil; production design

title: Charity is not hierarchy or spectacle
source_observation: The offering begins a mission directed toward hungry beings rather than elite display
risk: Lavish staging could centre donor purity wealth or praise over recipients and labour
decision: Keep the food materially plausible the gesture specific and the wish outward-facing without a worshipful donor tableau
rationale: Preserves the ethic that giving food is giving life
required_review: Food history; ethics; director; crowd and prop teams

title: Aadhirai scene remains connected to maritime and public systems
source_observation: Her story links merchant voyage shipwreck stranger protection household consequence and public feeding
risk: Compression could detach the alms scene from Sadhuvan's maritime history or overwhelm the main plot with a complete secondary film
decision: Use selected maritime and household beats only where they change the meaning of her offering and Manimekalai's mission
rationale: Preserves causal richness while controlling feature runtime
required_review: Screenplay architecture; maritime history; editor; classical Tamil
